content.text:
All 5 changes applied:

1. **`.so-card` desktop** — `padding: 1rem 0` → `1rem 0.75rem` (added horizontal padding)
2. **`.so-card` mobile** — `padding: 0.75rem 0` → `0.75rem 0.6rem`
3. **`.so-card--parent` mobile** — added `padding: 0.85rem` + `border-radius: 12px`
4. **`.opinions-page-parent-container .so-card-wrapper` mobile** — `width: 94%` → `92%`
5. **`.rc-reply` mobile** — added `gap: 0.45rem` (tighter reply threads)
6. **`.visited-profile-opinions-container` mobile** — added `width: 95%` + `padding: 0.65rem`

Perfect! I now have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive report of my findings:

## Summary: Draft References in iskrib_v3 Client Codebase

I've completed a thorough search of the client codebase for draft-related functionality. Here are my findings:

### 1. Route Path for Drafts

**Location:** `/home/drafts`

Defined in:
- **C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/App.jsx** (line 141)
  ```jsx
  <Route path='drafts' element={<DraftList/>}/>
  ```

### 2. Draft Navigation Link & Icon SVG

**Location:** C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx (lines 153-164)

**Navigation Object:**
```jsx
{
    path: '/home/drafts',
    label: 'Drafts',
    action: () => navigatePath('/home/drafts'),
    icon:
    <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="28px" height="28px" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill={pathname === '/home/drafts' ? activeColor : inactiveColor}>
        <path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8l-6-6z"/>
        <polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8" fill="none" stroke={pathname === '/home/drafts' ? activeColor : inactiveColor} strokeWidth="1"/>
        <line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13" stroke={pathname === '/home/drafts' ? '#fff' : inactiveColor} strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"/>
        <line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17" stroke={pathname === '/home/drafts' ? '#fff' : inactiveColor} strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"/>
    </svg>
}
```

**Icon Description:** Document/page icon with fold corner and two horizontal lines (representing text). Uses dynamic color based on active state (blue `#5f92ffff` when active, gray `#b6b6b6ff` when inactive).

### 3. Draft Icon Used Elsewhere

The same document icon appears in:

**C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/DraftList/DraftList.jsx** (lines 82-88)
- Empty state icon showing when no drafts exist:
```jsx
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="48" height="48" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1.5" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round">
    <path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z"/>
    <polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8"/>
    <line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13"/>
    <line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17"/>
    <polyline points="10 9 9 9 8 9"/>
</svg>
```

### 4. Key Implementation Details

**Component:** C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/DraftList/DraftList.jsx
- Displays all user drafts with title and edit timestamp
- Allows resuming drafts (opens in editor with content pre-loaded)
- Allows deleting drafts
- Shows empty state when no drafts

**API Endpoints:** C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/API/Api.js (lines 276-283)
```javascript
export const saveDraft = (token, body) =>
    authedJsonRequest(token, 'POST', '/journal/draft', body, 'failed to save draft');

export const publishDraft = (token, journalId) =>
    authedJsonRequest(token, 'POST', '/journal/publish', { journalId }, 'failed to publish draft');

export const getDrafts = (token) =>
    authedGet(token, '/journal/drafts', 'failed to fetch drafts');
```

**Editor Integration:** C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/RichTextEditor.jsx
- Auto-saves drafts every 30 seconds while editing
- Tracks draft status (saving/saved/unsaved)
- Can publish draft directly to journal
- Supports resuming draft editing with full content reload

### 5. Mobile Sidebar Status

**Note:** The mobile sidebar (C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/MobileSidebarLink/MobileSidebarLink.jsx) currently does NOT include a drafts link. It only includes: Notifications, Bookmarks, Analytics, and Settings.

This is a missing feature for mobile parity with the desktop sidebar which has the Drafts link.

input.content:
# Add Drafts Button to Mobile Sidebar ## Context The desktop sidebar in `Home.jsx` has a Drafts link (`/home/drafts`), but the mobile sidebar (`MobileSidebarLink.jsx`) is missing it. This adds parity. ## Changes ### File: `client/src/components/MobileSidebarLink/MobileSidebarLink.jsx` Add a Drafts nav item between Bookmarks and Analytics (matching desktop order). Uses the same SVG icon and route path as the desktop sidebar. ```jsx
navigatePath('/home/drafts')} className='sidebar-nav-container'>
Drafts
``` Insert after the Bookmarks `` (line 82) and before the Analytics `
` (line 84). No CSS changes needed — reuses existing `.sidebar-nav-container`, `.sidebar-nav-bttn`, and `.sidebar-nav-bttn-active` classes. ## Verification 1. Open mobile sidebar (< 480px) — Drafts appears between Bookmarks and Analytics 2. Tap Drafts — navigates to `/home/drafts` and sidebar closes 3. Active state: amber icon when on `/home/drafts` 4. Desktop — no change (mobile sidebar is `display: none` above 480px)

lines: -# Fix Mobile Spacing — Opinions Cards Too Flush to Screen Edges
+# Add Drafts Button to Mobile Sidebar

-After the borderless timeline redesign, `.so-card` lost its horizontal padding (`padding: 1rem 0`), and on mobile the cards sit flush against the screen edges. The content needs breathing room (horizontal padding) so it doesn't feel cramped on small screens.
+The desktop sidebar in `Home.jsx` has a Drafts link (`/home/drafts`), but the mobile sidebar (`MobileSidebarLink.jsx`) is missing it. This adds parity.
-- `.opinions-page-parent-container .so-card-wrapper` is `width: 94%` on mobile but the card itself has no internal padding
-- `.so-card--parent` (opinion viewer) has `padding: 1.25rem` which is fine, but needs a mobile reduction
-- `.visited-profile-opinions-container` has `padding: 1rem` which is fine but the cards inside have no padding
+Add a Drafts nav item between Bookmarks and Analytics (matching desktop order). Uses the same SVG icon and route path as the desktop sidebar.
+            <line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13" stroke={location.pathname === '/home/drafts' ? '#fff' : "#b6b6b6"} strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"/>
+            <line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17" stroke={location.pathname === '/home/drafts' ? '#fff' : "#b6b6b6"} strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"/>

+Insert after the Bookmarks `` (line 82) and before the Analytics `
` (line 84).
-### 3. Ensure opinion viewer wrapper has horizontal padding on mobile
-The opinion viewer renders inside the same `.opinions-page-parent-container` or route wrapper. The `.so-card-wrapper` width of 94% on mobile is fine but could use a small bump:
-        width: 92%;  /* was 94%, gives more edge breathing room */
+No CSS changes needed — reuses existing `.sidebar-nav-container`, `.sidebar-nav-bttn`, and `.sidebar-nav-bttn-active` classes.
-The `.rc-reply` content also needs small padding within the `.rc-content` block, and the `.rc-inline-composer` textarea should not touch the edges.
-        gap: 0.45rem;  /* was 0.65rem, save space on small screens */
-### 5. Visited profile opinions container — tighten on mobile
-- `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/sidebarOpinions.css` — CSS-only changes in desktop defaults + `@media(max-width: 480px)` block
-1. OpinionsPage on mobile (< 480px): cards have visible horizontal padding, text doesn't touch screen edges
-2. OpinionViewer on mobile: parent card has padding, reply composer has breathing room
-3. Reply threads on mobile: content doesn't touch edges, nested threads still indent properly
-4. Visited profile opinions on mobile: glass container and cards have spacing
-5. Desktop: minimal visual change (slight horizontal padding added, looks intentional)
-6. Sidebar context: unchanged (sidebar already has container padding from `.latest-opinions-container`)
+1. Open mobile sidebar (< 480px) — Drafts appears between Bookmarks and Analytics
+2. Tap Drafts — navigates to `/home/drafts` and sidebar closes
+3. Active state: amber icon when on `/home/drafts`
+4. Desktop — no change (mobile sidebar is `display: none` above 480px)
